name: E6 grand unified theory
summary: Embeds the Standard Model gauge group in the exceptional Lie group E6, providing a unified framework for all SM gauge interactions plus additional matter. Each fermion family fits into a 27-dimensional representation, producing two extra heavy quark-doublet partners and additional neutral leptons per family. Naturally arises in heterotic string compactifications on Calabi-Yau manifolds.

addresses_puzzles.note: Gauge couplings unify cleanly under E6 only with matter content beyond minimal SM (typically requires SUSY); pure SM running into E6 misses unification. Predicts proton decay at long lifetimes and dark-matter candidates among extra light states.
27 contains an SM-singlet neutrino per family that can serve as the right-handed neutrino in the type-I seesaw, with M_R set by the GUT-scale physics.
E6 contains SO(10) and adds further unification of matter; reduces the family Yukawa structure further (Gursey-Ramond-Sikivie 1976).

touches_benchmarks.note: E6 GUT predicts dimension-6 p -> e+ pi^0 at rates near Super-K bounds for M_GUT ~ 10^16 GeV; Hyper-K reach will further constrain or detect.

new_fields: additional matter beyond SM 16-plet (one E6 27 contains 27 chiral fermions per family)
introduces.note: GUT scale, GUT gauge coupling, family structure, soft / breaking parameters, intermediate-scale matter.

history: Gursey, Ramond, Sikivie (1976), 'A universal gauge theory model based on E6', Phys. Lett. B 60, 177. Re-emerged in heterotic string contexts (Candelas-Horowitz-Strominger-Witten 1985); the 27 representation aligns naturally with Calabi-Yau matter content. Moderate phenomenological activity through 1990s-2000s, mostly tied to specific string constructions.

references.citation: Gursey, Ramond, Sikivie, 'A universal gauge theory model based on E6', Phys. Lett. B 60, 177 (1976)
